title: Western Water Accelerated Revenue Repayment Act
short_title: Western Water Accelerated Revenue Repayment Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.
plain_summary: This bill makes a water payment option permanent for people and businesses in western states who get water from government projects. Instead of making regular payments over many years, water users can now choose to pay off their entire water contract early with one big payment or spread it over three years. When people make these early payments, some of that money must go into a special account that helps build new water storage projects like dams and reservoirs. The bill keeps the same rules about where certain payments have to go based on older laws that were already in place.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Western Water Accelerated Revenue Repayment Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Amendments to the WIIN Act
sections.text: This section changes two parts of an existing law called the Water Infrastructure Improvements for the Nation Act. The first change adds new language about money that must go to specific accounts instead of the General Reclamation Fund. This only applies to money that older laws already said had to go to those other accounts before this new law was passed. The second change extends prepayment authority. This means it adds section four thousand eleven to the list of sections where people can pay off their water contracts early.
